label: escalate-target
ground_truth_notes: Network device (border router) showing intermittent BGP session resets and a small unexplained CPU spike pattern. Vendor firmware. No agent, no host-side telemetry, no out-of-band attestation tooling deployed. Defender will likely lean benign (firmware bug, ISP flap). The right critic move is ESCALATE: firmware-tamper class is real but the environment has no observable consequence to test against. CRITIQUE here would require fabricating a check (e.g., 'verify firmware hash' — but the alert says no such tooling exists).

alert.device: rtr-edge-01.dc1 (vendor: Acme NetworkOS, model X9000, firmware 7.4.2)
events: BGP session reset to upstream-isp-1 at 02:14, 04:31, 06:08 (Tuesday); previous 30d had 1 such reset
control-plane CPU spike to 78% for 14s at 02:14:03Z (typical baseline 8-15%)
no config changes in last 60d (last change: 2025-03-18 by netops-team)
context: device is vendor closed firmware; no host agent, no syscall telemetry, no log forwarding beyond syslog
syslog from device only contains operational events emitted by vendor code; no integrity attestation
no firmware-attestation tooling (e.g., signed-boot verifier, vendor TAM) deployed in this environment
no OOB management network instrumentation beyond reachability ping
vendor advisory page lists no CVE for firmware 7.4.2 affecting BGP stability
ISP confirms no upstream link flaps in their NOC log during the alert windows
control plane CPU on this model normally spikes only on config commit or full BGP table reconvergence; neither occurred
